role: Accumulator — return value and syscall number; implicit operand for mul/div/in/out.
4th integer argument (System V AMD64 ABI); caller-saved. Traditionally the loop/shift counter (loop, rep, shl/shr, ...).
3rd integer argument; caller-saved. Also the implicit high half for mul/div (RDX:RAX).
2nd integer argument; caller-saved. Traditionally the source pointer for string instructions (movs, cmps, lods).
1st integer argument; caller-saved. Traditionally the destination pointer for string instructions (movs, stos, cmps, scas).
Callee-saved; traditionally the stack frame base pointer.
The stack pointer — always points at the top of the stack.
Caller-saved. In a syscall specifically, holds the 4th argument (rcx can't be used there — the syscall instruction itself overwrites it).
Caller-saved; overwritten by the syscall instruction itself (holds the saved RFLAGS).
